codeReview.summary: Compared to the original failed implementation (6b14104), this fix commit addresses both blocking gaps: gap-repair now paginates indexed conversation documents instead of stopping at a fixed first slice, and missing source conversations now enqueue purge jobs so orphaned search artifacts are removed. The new DataStore/SearchIndexStore pagination overloads are wired into enqueueGapRepairIfNeeded, and delete-miss recovery behavior is covered by dedicated tests.

issues.description: The test named test_gapRepair_paginatesFullCorpus_beyondInitialSlice uses only 25 conversations while production gap-repair page size is 1000, so the test does not actually cross a pagination boundary. This weakens regression protection for true multi-page traversal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The indexing-efficiency skill requires running retrieval evals when projection/retrieval output changes, but this worker handoff only recorded xcodebuild + OpenBurnBarCore tests for this projection-path change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md:32-35 requires scripts/test-openburnbar-retrieval-evals.sh when projection output changed; handoffs/2026-04-09T05-16-40-814Z__m3-fix-gap-repair-pagination-and-delete-purge__cb7febba-84de-4153-a12a-b3ee39883513.json:14-28 lists commandsRun without that retrieval-eval command.

summary: Reviewed prior failed review, fix transcript skeleton, fix handoff, and both commit diffs (6b14104 and 33cb58a). The fix adequately resolves the original blocking failures (full-corpus sweep pagination and missing-source purge recovery), so this review is pass with one non-blocking pagination-boundary test-coverage note.
